Methods to overcome sparsity problem: "Since some players developed less than the standard 20 solutions required to complete the game, which may or may not have resulted in them winning the game, we employed two techniques to create a stationary submission set for each player. The paper mentions an imbalance between positive and negative samples in the dataset and addresses it by using the Area Under the Curve (AUC) as a performance metric to evaluate model performance. AUC is robust against sample class distribution imbalances.

Addresses bias: "In the novel contrastive loss function, distinct weights are assigned to global and local data, facilitating deep knowledge tracing models in learning from global data information. This strategy helps balance the model's focus between students with varying levels of interaction with skills." 

The paper addresses this bias by introducing a contrastive learning strategy that aims to balance the model's focus between students with different levels of interaction.

The paper mentions potential bias in the manually-labeled Q-matrix due to inevitable errors and subjective bias. The authors address this by defining an enhanced Q-matrix to mitigate possible omissions or mistakes. They also acknowledge inaccuracies in clustering results and plan to use pre-trained representations to improve data accuracy.

"Traditionally, if the knowledge concept k m is not contained in the exercise e j , Q jm will be set as 0, which shows students' performance on exercise e j has no influence on their knowledge mastery h m on knowledge concept k m . However, manually-labeled Q-matrix may be deficient because of inevitable errors and subjective bias [15,36]. In order to make up for possible omissions or mistakes, we define an enhanced Q-matrix q _ R J _M , where q jm will be set as a small positive value _ rather than 0 even if k m is not in e j . Although this unified setting is simple as well, as the focus of our paper is on the learning process modeling part, we leave the exploration to learn the specific weights in the Q-matrix as future works."

The paper addresses data sparsity by constructing KC relation graphs based on data statistics and setting thresholds to ensure significant relationships are considered. "In the absence of KC relation annotations in the datasets, we construct the KC relation graph based on data statistics."

ten variants of MPSG are designed to investigate the effectiveness of each key component. The ablation studies mainly focus on four aspects: (1) the sampling process, (2) the graph networks for representation learning, (3) the feature aggregation strategies, and (4) the constraints.

The paper addresses "answer bias," which is an unbalanced distribution of correct and incorrect answers for each question. 

The answer bias can be naturally explained in terms of the differences in difficulty between questions (Shen et al., 2022). Nevertheless, it also provides a shortcut for achieving good prediction performance in KT. After a preliminary analysis, we found that a dumb model that blindly selects the more frequently occurring answer for each question can also achieve reasonable accuracy in testing, even without taking into account any of the students� past learning interactions!

They provide density metrics for each dataset and show how their approach helps with "alleviating the problem of sparse courses."
